home.meta_title: Fuel Economy Calculator - Save on Fuel | FuelEcon Australia
home.meta_description: Calculate fuel costs in AUD, compare vehicle efficiency in L/100km, and find fuel-saving tips for Australian drivers.
home.ev_notice_html: ⚡ Electric vehicle? Compare EV vs Petrol savings

faq.q: How does FuelEcon calculate trip costs?
faq.a: We take your vehicle's fuel economy (L/100km), the total distance of your trip in kilometres, and the current fuel price to work out exactly how many litres you'll need and what they will cost.

Your car has a rated fuel economy, but things like low tyre pressure or driving on stop-start city roads instead of the freeway make it less efficient. Peak efficiency is when you're getting the best possible range from your fuel.

They are very close for most drivers! We use official fuel economy averages and live fuel prices. However, things like heavy cargo, aggressive driving, or very hot weather (using lots of A/C) can change your real results.

faq.q: Why does tyre pressure matter for fuel?
faq.a: Low tyres create more 'rolling resistance,' meaning your engine has to work harder to move the car. Keeping them inflated to the recommended level can save you about 3% to 10% on fuel.

faq.q: Is it better to drive with windows down or A/C on?
faq.a: Below 80 km/h, windows down is usually better. At freeway speeds, the 'drag' from open windows actually costs more fuel than running the A/C.

faq.q: How much can I save by driving slower?
faq.a: For most cars, fuel economy drops significantly above 100 km/h. Every 10 km/h you drive over 100 is like paying an extra 20-30 cents per litre.

faq.q: Does idling use a lot of fuel?
faq.a: Yes. If you are stopped for more than 30 seconds (and not in traffic), it's usually cheaper to turn the engine off and restart it than to let it idle.
On flat highways, yes. It helps maintain a steady speed, which is much more efficient than constant micro-adjustments with your foot.

faq.q: Why do roof racks affect fuel economy?
faq.a: They break the aerodynamic shape of your car. Even an empty roof rack can reduce your efficiency by 2% to 5% because of wind resistance.
The Green Zone is our way of showing you're driving at peak efficiency. When your route and tyres are optimised, you're in the Green Zone!

faq.q: Can I save my car for later?
faq.a: Yes! Use the 'Save this car' button to keep your vehicle's info in your browser so you don't have to type it in next time.

faq.q: Where do you get fuel prices?
faq.a: We use regional averages from official Australian sources and retail price series.

tips: 🌿 Below 80 km/h, open windows instead of A/C — A/C can cost 3-4 MPG.
🛣️ Steady 90-100 km/h on the freeway is the fuel economy sweet spot for most cars.
📦 Roof racks tank fuel economy — take them off when you're not using them.
⛽ Cruise control on flat highway = 7-14% fuel economy improvement.
🔧 A clogged air filter can cost you 10% fuel economy. Change it when dirty.
🚗 Combine errands — cold starts use 2x more fuel per km than warm driving.
🕐 Idling over 30 seconds burns more fuel than restarting the engine.
🏔️ Drive down hills in gear, not neutral — modern cars cut fuel when engine-braking.

ecoAdvice.highwaySaving: 🛣️ Take the highway if you can — you'd save about A${savings} on this exact trip.
ecoAdvice.highwayOk: ✅ Highway route — already the cheapest option for fuel.
ecoAdvice.tiresLow: ⚠️ Your tyres are low. Inflating them is free and gives you back about 8% fuel economy.
ecoAdvice.acceleration: 📉 Smooth acceleration = 10-30% less fuel over a tank.

evVsGas.heading: Is an EV Worth It?
evVsGas.result_never: EV never pays back under these assumptions
evVsGas.result_explanation: At the break-even point, fuel savings equal the EV's higher upfront cost.
